ChangeRequest.name: [Asset Sec] New entities required: ABCP Programme and ABCP securitisation
ChangeRequest.comment:
See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab)
# Overview Asset-backed commercial papers (ABCP) securitisations consist of 2 levels: the ABCP programme (sometimes called conduit) and the ABCP transaction. There can be a many to many relationship between them. In all COREP reporting, but also ESMA, the securitisations reported should have one of the following types: ABCP Programme, ABCP transaction, Traditional non-ABCP, Synthetic. The type of securitisation in COREP can therefore be derived from the subtype of the entity where the securitisation is defined. At the moment, not all requirements for ABCP are clear but we can start creating the entities and the relationships. I will complete the other requirements later.
### Contact person olivia.hauet@ecb.europa.eu arjan.bos@ecb.europa.eu
### Business definition _[Business requirements]_
### Legal definition Article 2 of the SECR (7) ‘asset-backed commercial paper programme’ or ‘ABCP programme’ means a programme of securitisations the securities issued by which predominantly take the form of asset-backed commercial paper with an original maturity of one year or less; (8) ‘asset-backed commercial paper transaction’ or ‘ABCP transaction’ means a securitisation within an ABCP programme;
### Reason for inclusion COREP
### Relationships ABCP Programme ABCP Transaction
### Relevant Framework/Template C 14.00, C 14.01, C 13.01
### Additional documentation/links (optional) _[Additional info]_
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
